title: Bridge Round: Chunk to Meaning
subject: literacy_intervention

room_setup: Display three headers: decode only, decode plus prove meaning, not enough evidence yet.

launch: Read the word accurately first, then prove what it means.
A point does not count unless the group can defend the meaning signal.
between_rounds: Push past the first chunk you notice.
debrief: Which item was easy to read but harder to prove?
Where did a word part help the most?

student_directions: Read the word or prompt accurately first.
Sort it under the best header and defend the choice with a meaning clue.
Revise if your proof does not hold.

round_sequence.label: Round 1
round_sequence.teacher_move: Start with a clean foundational confidence builder.

round_sequence.label: Round 2
round_sequence.teacher_move: Require decode plus prove, not decoding alone.

round_sequence.label: Round 3
round_sequence.teacher_move: Bridge into a familiar prefix round.

round_sequence.label: Round 4
round_sequence.teacher_move: Finish with a stronger academic word.

debrief_questions: Which rounds felt more like decoding?
Which rounds needed stronger meaning proof?

whiteboard.label: Whiteboard bridge
notes: Students show the category and proof on whiteboards instead of sorting cards.
prompt_adjustments: Use one word at a time.

easier.label: Easier bridge
notes: Use only starter and core items.
prompt_adjustments: Keep the headers visible the whole time.

harder.label: Harder bridge
notes: Add one arguable or near-miss trap.
Require a second example after each correct proof.
prompt_adjustments: Do not tell students which strand the item comes from.

worksheet_companion.label: Bridge recording strip
notes: Students record the word, the chosen header, and the proof signal after each round.
prompt_adjustments: Use after oral defense, not before.

extension_questions: Which words were easy to read but harder to prove?

exit_slip.prompt: Write one word from today and prove its meaning with a chunk or word-part clue.

teacher_notes: This bridge only works if you insist on proof after the reading move.
Keep the transition from foundational to morphology visible in the debrief.
differentiation_notes: Use the easier path when students still need visible category headers.
Use the harder path only when students are ready for borderline cases.
curricular_links: This bridge links foundational word reading with morphology-based meaning work in Grade 6-8 literacy intervention.
